# dreamd — Full Documentation for LLMs

## What dreamd is

dreamd makes Claude Code, Cursor, and Cline remember the same things. Drop a `.agent/`
folder in your repo, run `npx dreamd-mcp`, and every MCP-aware coding agent reads and
writes the same memory — episodic events, lessons, preferences — checked into git
alongside your code.

AGENTS.md is what you wrote down. dreamd is what your agent learned, across every tool.

The problem dreamd solves: developers now run 4+ coding agents in rotation. Every one
of them has amnesia about your codebase. Claude Code learns something Tuesday afternoon;
Cursor has no idea Friday morning. dreamd is the fix — a shared memory layer all of them
read and write via MCP, without any of them needing to know the others exist.

## What dreamd is not

- Not a vector database. v0.1 uses BM25 lexical recall (Tantivy). Semantic/embedding
  recall is v0.2.
- Not a hosted SaaS. v0.1 makes zero network calls. Everything stays on your machine.
- Not a replacement for AGENTS.md or SKILL.md. Those are human-authored project rules;
  `.agent/` is machine-written runtime memory. They complement each other.
- Not a Python SDK. No Python required. Distribution is npm (`npx`) and Rust (`cargo`).
- Not competing with Mem0, Letta, or Zep. Those are SDKs you embed in one agent you
  control. dreamd is a memory layer multiple existing agents share via MCP — a
  different problem entirely. None of them has a cross-harness story.

## Install

### No Rust required (v0.1 path)

  npx @dataprime1/dreamd-mcp init   # scaffold .agent/ in your project
  npx @dataprime1/dreamd-mcp        # start the MCP server (stdio)

Supported: Linux x86_64/aarch64, macOS x86_64/aarch64. Node >= 18 required.
Windows arrives in v0.1.1.

### From source (requires Rust stable)

  git clone https://github.com/botzrDev/dreamd.git
  cd dreamd
  cargo install --path crates/dreamd-cli
  dreamd init
  dreamd mcp

## MCP configuration

### Claude Code — add to `.mcp.json` in your project root:

  {
    "mcpServers": {
      "dreamd": {
        "command": "npx",
        "args": ["-y", "@dataprime1/dreamd-mcp"]
      }
    }
  }

### Cursor — same `.mcp.json` config. Also copy
  `adapters/cursor/.cursor/rules/dreamd-recall.mdc`
  into your project's `.cursor/rules/` for automatic recall on session start.

### Cline / other MCP-aware harnesses — same `.mcp.json` config applies.

## MCP tools

dreamd exposes two MCP tools: `search_nodes` and `append_node`.

### search_nodes — recall from memory

  Input:  { "query": "string", "k": number }   (k defaults to 10)
  Output: ranked list of episodic events, scored by BM25 × salience

  Use when:
  - Starting a new task in a project worked in before
  - The user references a past decision or problem
  - About to make a choice that may have a documented prior

### append_node — write to memory

  Input:
  {
    "skill_action":   "rust::error_handling::axum_rejection",  // :: -separated, language-first
    "content":        "Axum requires custom Error types to implement IntoResponse.",
    "source_harness": "claude-code",   // required — omitting causes a deserialization error
    "pain":           7.5,             // 0–10, friction caused by this problem
    "importance":     8.0,             // 0–10, how often this pattern recurs
    "client_dedup_key": "axum_requires_custom_error_types_to_implement_intoresponse"
      // optional; first 60 chars of content, lowercased, spaces → underscores
  }
  Returns: 201 after fdatasync

  skill_action convention: language::domain::specific
    rust::error_handling::axum_rejection
    typescript::testing::vitest_async_timeout
    python::deps::virtualenv_activation

PROTOCOL NOTE: after `initialize`, send `notifications/initialized` before calling any
tools. Without it, `search_nodes` returns empty results silently.

## .agent/ folder layout

  .agent/
    episodic/AGENT_LEARNINGS.jsonl   # append-only event log
    semantic/LESSONS.md              # lessons consolidated by the dream cycle
    personal/PREFERENCES.md          # user preferences (never sent to LLM without consent)
    working/                         # reserved for v0.2
    .dreamd/                         # implementation state — gitignored

All files are UTF-8 plaintext. `.agent/` is checked into git. You can cat, grep,
git diff, and hand-edit any file. The agent reads your edits.

## Episodic record schema (schema_version: "1.0.0")

  {
    "schema_version": "1.0.0",
    "id":             "evt_01hz8k2x",
    "timestamp":      "2026-05-08T10:55:00Z",
    "source_harness": "claude-code",
    "skill_action":   "rust::error_handling::axum_rejection",
    "content":        "Axum requires custom Error types to implement IntoResponse.",
    "pain":           7.5,
    "importance":     8.0,
    "pinned":         false
  }

## CLI commands

  dreamd init              # scaffold .agent/ in the current project
  dreamd mcp               # start MCP server over stdio
  dreamd watch             # foreground daemon mode (SIGINT/SIGTERM-graceful)
  dreamd dream             # run the dream cycle: consolidate episodic → semantic
  dreamd dream --dry       # preview what the dream cycle would do
  dreamd doctor            # health check
  dreamd version           # structured version output
  dreamd reset workspace   # re-scaffold working/WORKSPACE.md

## Recall latency (v0.1, warm BM25 index, Criterion 0.5, WSL2/Linux)

  1,000 entries    ~50 µs
  10,000 entries   ~313 µs
  100,000 entries  ~2.8 ms

All well under the <5ms P50 warm target. Run `cargo bench -p dreamd-core` to reproduce.
Read-after-write visibility: up to commit_cadence_seconds (default 5s).

## Privacy

v0.1 makes zero network calls. `personal/PREFERENCES.md` is never sent to any LLM.
When LLM-assisted dream cycle lands in v0.1.1, episodic content above the salience
threshold may be sent to the configured provider — opt-in, `personal/` excluded by
default unless you explicitly pass `--share-personal`.

## v0.1 scope (do not promise beyond this)

IN v0.1: BM25 lexical recall, Linux + macOS, deterministic dream cycle, npm distribution.
v0.1.1: semantic/embedding recall, Windows, LLM-assisted dream cycle, Homebrew/cargo install.
